title: Gently Smoky Ukrainian Fish Pilaf
description: A smoky and creamy Ukrainian dish featuring pistachio paste, perfect for a brunch mood, pescatarian-friendly and delicious.
instructions: Rinse the rice in a fine mesh strainer until the water runs clear, then drain and set aside.
In a large saucepan, melt 1 tablespoon of butter over medium heat.
Add the smoked paprika, salt, and black pepper, and stir for 1 minute.
Add the rice to the saucepan and stir to coat the rice with the spice mixture.
Add the chicken broth to the saucepan, bring to a boil, then reduce the heat to low, cover, and simmer for 18 minutes.
While the rice cooks, prepare the cod fillets by seasoning with salt and pepper.
In a large skillet, melt the remaining 1 tablespoon of butter over medium heat, then add the cod fillets and cook for 4 minutes per side, or until cooked through.
Stir the pistachio paste into the cooked rice, then flake the cooked cod into the rice mixture and stir to combine.
Serve the pilaf hot, garnished with chopped parsley and a lemon wedge on the side.
